body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) closed at 7,559.66, up from 7,529.98 at the opening bell, driven by the growth of blue-chip stocks such as PT Barito Renewables Energy TBK and PT Bank Central Asia TBK. The positive trend also lifted other indices, including LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL, with some stocks like PT Unilever Indonesia TBK and PT Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur TBK showing uptrends.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Closes Higher with Gains in Blue-Chip Stocks

body: Indonesia's foreign debts from international organizations totaled USD 46.07 billion, with the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development being the largest creditor, accounting for 46.69% of the total. The country's overall foreign debts stood at USD 425.05 billion in August 2024, up 2.35% from the previous month and 7.3% higher than the same period last year.
title: Indonesia's foreign debt reaches USD 425.05 billion in August 2024, with international organizations and countries like Singapore and China being major creditors.

body: PT Energy Transporter Indonesia, a subsidiary of PT Transcoal Pacific Tbk, has divested its barge ETI 800 for IDR 6 billion to an unaffiliated company, aiming to raise funds for the restoration of its other vessels. The divestment is expected to improve the operational activities of ETI in the future, as the sold vessel had become less commercially viable to operate.
title: PT Energy Transporter Indonesia sells barge ETI 800 for IDR 6 billion to raise funds for vessel restoration.

body: As of the first half of 2024, the total users' funds in People's Economic Bank reached IDR 167.1 trillion, a 6.1% year-on-year increase from IDR 157.42 trillion in the same period in 2023. The majority of savings, 98.22%, were under IDR 100 million, with a total value of IDR 46.62 trillion spread across 15.81 million accounts.
title: Total Users' Funds in People's Economic Bank Reach IDR 167.1 Trillion in First Half 2024

body: PT Barito Renewables Energy TBK's (BREN) stock price surged 3.85% to IDR 6,750 per share, with a market capitalization of IDR 896.37 trillion and a price-to-earning ratio of 532.14. The stock's upward trend also lifted the Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index, with other large-cap stocks, including PT Barito Pacific TBK, PT Bank Central Asia TBK, and PT Bank Negara Indonesia TBK, also experiencing gains.
title: PT Barito Renewables Energy's stock price surges 3.85% to IDR 6,750 per share, leading Jakarta's stock market capitalization.

body: PT Gunung Raja Paksi Tbk will reduce its share nominal value from IDR 500 to IDR 140, effective November 5, 2024, following approval from the Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders and the Minister of Law and Human Rights. The company will refund IDR 360 per share on November 8, 2024, and trading with the new nominal value will begin on November 5, 2024, with the last transaction at the old nominal value set for October 31, 2024.
title: Gunung Raja Paksi to Reduce Nominal Share Value from IDR 500 to IDR 140 Effective November 5, 2024.

body: The Indonesian government will hold an auction of government securities next Tuesday, aiming to raise IDR 22 trillion to meet its 2024 state budget financing target. This year, the government has conducted 18 SUN auctions, with a total awarded amount of IDR 415.61 trillion, and the upcoming auction will offer 7 series of securities with yields ranging from 6.50% to 7.12% per annum.
title: Indonesian Government to Hold Auction for Government Securities Worth IDR 22 Trillion Next Tuesday

body: PT Daaz Bara Lestari Tbk, a commodity trading company, is set to raise up to IDR 270 billion in its initial public offering (IPO) by issuing 300 million shares, equivalent to 15.02% of its total issued and paid-up capital, at a price range of IDR 835-900 per share. The book building process is scheduled for October 11-18, 2024, followed by a public offering from November 1-7, 2024, and listing on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on November 11, 2024.
title: Daaz Bara Lestari to Raise Up to IDR 270 Billion in IPO with 300 Million Shares Offering

body: PT Net Visi Media Tbk has undergone a change in its directors and commissioners following the acquisition of 80.05% of its shares by PT MD Entertainment Tbk. Manoj Dhamoo Punjabi has been appointed as the new President Director, replacing Deddy Hariyanto, while other new directors and commissioners have also been assigned, including Priyadharsi Anand and Shania Manoj Punjabi.
title: PT Net Visi Media TBK appoints new President Director and Commissioners following acquisition by PT MD Entertainment TBK.

body: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara TBK (TOWR) plans to raise IDR 4.5 trillion through a rights issue, with proceeds allocated to refinance its subsidiary's loan and working capital, and the company's authorized capital will increase to IDR 2.24 trillion. The rights issue will result in a maximum 9.12% dilution of ownership for shareholders who do not exercise their rights, and the company's issued paid-up capital will expand to IDR 560.14 billion, or 25% of the authorized capital.
title: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara to Raise IDR 4.5 Trillion through Rights Issue to Refinance Subsidiary's Obligations.

body: PT Fortune Indonesia Tbk is exploring new business opportunities in steel manufacturing, mining infrastructure services, heavy equipment leasing, and well mining, which are expected to provide added value to shareholders. Despite a 28.7% year-on-year revenue increase to IDR 10.49 billion in Q2 2024, the company still reported a loss of IDR 1.4 billion.
title: PT Fortune Indonesia Tbk Explores New Business Opportunities Beyond Media Printing

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) plummeted to the red zone, closing at 7,478.17, with a slight gain from its worst position at 7,472.95, after briefly soaring above 7,500 earlier in the day. The decline affected other indices, including LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESG, with several blue-chip stocks, such as PT Bank Central Asia and PT Indofood Sukses Makmur, experiencing corrections.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Plunges into Red Zone Despite Brief Morning Rally
